words.definitionEn: money in the form of coins or notes
words.exampleEn: How much cash do you have on you?

words.definitionEn: a large strong building with thick, high walls and towers, built in the past by kings or queens, or other important people, to defend themselves against attack
words.exampleEn: a medieval castle

words.definitionEn: used in speaking to emphasize a fact or a comment, or that something is really true
words.exampleEn: What did she actually say?

words.definitionEn: to stop and hold a moving object or person, especially in your hands
words.exampleEn: She managed to catch the keys as they fell.

words.definitionEn: the person or thing that makes something happen
words.exampleEn: Unemployment is a major cause of poverty.

words.definitionEn: to make something happen, especially something bad or unpleasant; to make somebody do something
words.exampleEn: cause something, Do they know what caused the fire?

words.definitionEn: to show that a day or an event is important by doing something special on it
words.exampleEn: Jake's passed his exams. We're going out to celebrate.

words.definitionEn: a famous person
words.exampleEn: a celebrity chef

words.definitionEn: strongly believing something; having no doubts
words.exampleEn: I think it was him, but I can't be certain.

words.definitionEn: without doubt
words.exampleEn: Without treatment, she will almost certainly die.

words.definitionEn: a possibility of something happening, especially something that you want
words.exampleEn: chance of doing something, She only has a slim chance of passing the exam.

words.definitionEn: a person or an animal in a book, play or film
words.exampleEn: the main character in the film

words.definitionEn: an organization for helping people in need
words.exampleEn: a registered charity

words.definitionEn: a friendly informal conversation
words.exampleEn: I just called in for a chat.

words.definitionEn: to talk in a friendly, informal way to somebody
words.exampleEn: I'm sorry I can't stop to chat.

words.definitionEn: an act of making sure that something is safe, correct or in good condition by examining it
words.exampleEn: Could you give the tyres a check?

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to cook, especially the most senior person in a restaurant, hotel, etc.
words.exampleEn: a new book by celebrity chef Jamie Oliver

words.definitionEn: the scientific study of the structure of substances, how they react when combined or in contact with one another, and how they behave under different conditions
words.exampleEn: a degree in chemistry

words.definitionEn: a long thin piece of potato fried in oil or fat
words.exampleEn: He was eating a burger and chips.

words.definitionEn: an act of choosing between two or more possibilities; something that you can choose
words.exampleEn: We are faced with a difficult choice.

words.definitionEn: a building where Christians go to attend services, pray, etc.
words.exampleEn: The procession moved into the church.

words.definitionEn: a thin tube of paper filled with tobacco, for smoking
words.exampleEn: to smoke/have a cigarette

words.definitionEn: a completely round flat shape
words.exampleEn: the centre/circumference/radius/diameter of a circle

words.definitionEn: to draw a circle around something
words.exampleEn: circle something, Luke circled a date on the kitchen calendar.

words.definitionEn: relating to classical music
words.exampleEn: a classical composer/musician/pianist

words.definitionEn: easy to understand and not confusing
words.exampleEn: She gave me clear and precise directions.

words.definitionEn: in a way that is easy to see or hear
words.exampleEn: Please speak clearly after the tone.

words.definitionEn: quick at learning and understanding things
words.exampleEn: a clever child

words.definitionEn: the regular pattern of weather conditions of a particular place
words.exampleEn: a tropical/warm/mild/temperate/cold climate

words.definitionEn: near in space or time
words.exampleEn: I had no idea the beach was so close.

words.definitionEn: shut
words.exampleEn: Keep the door closed.

words.definitionEn: clothes, especially a particular type of clothes
words.exampleEn: Workers at the factory wear protective clothing.

words.definitionEn: a grey or white mass that floats in the sky, made of very small drops of water
words.exampleEn: Dark clouds were gathering in the west.

words.definitionEn: a person who trains a person or team in sport
words.exampleEn: the head/assistant coach

words.definitionEn: the land next to or near to the sea or ocean
words.exampleEn: a trip to the coast

words.definitionEn: a system of words, letters, numbers or symbols that represent a message or record information secretly or in a shorter form
words.exampleEn: to break/crack a code (= to understand and read the message)

words.definitionEn: a person that you work with, especially in a profession or a business
words.exampleEn: work/senior/male colleagues

words.definitionEn: to bring things together from different people or places
words.exampleEn: collect something, to collect data/evidence/information

words.definitionEn: one of the straight sections from top to bottom into which text on a page or screen is divided
words.exampleEn: a dictionary with two columns per page

words.definitionEn: a play, film or TV show that is intended to be funny, usually with a happy ending; plays, films and TV shows of this type
words.exampleEn: a romantic comedy

words.definitionEn: making you feel physically relaxed; pleasant to wear, sit on, etc.
words.exampleEn: It's such a comfortable bed.

words.definitionEn: something that you say or write that gives an opinion on or explains somebody/something
words.exampleEn: comment on something, She made helpful comments on my work.

words.definitionEn: to share or exchange information, news, ideas, feelings, etc.
words.exampleEn: We only communicate by email.

words.definitionEn: all the people who live in a particular area, country, etc. when talked about as a group
words.exampleEn: The local community supported us from the start.

words.definitionEn: to take part in a contest or game
words.exampleEn: The games were an outstanding success, with almost 2 400 athletes competing.
